latestCycle.goal: reduce the dominant sample0Ms cost inside WindStreamlineRenderer._evolveParticles without breaking the live solo path
latestCycle.verifiedImprovement: adding an in-range bilinear fast path plus scratch-sample reuse in WindStreamlineRenderer reduced the mature live Earth.update p95 from 42.01 ms on the recent sample0 baseline to 34.20 ms and then 33.39 ms on two fresh reruns, while lowering sample0Ms p95 from 2.60 ms to 2.30 ms on the confirming rerun; the overall smoothness gate still fails because larger unknown max spikes and weak wind targets remain

blockingGaps: northern subtropical dry-belt moisture partitioning still fails in both fresh quick and seasonal audits, though the gap is now smaller at 1.029 at 30 days and 1.015 at 90 days

browser/runtime signoff is still blocked by larger remaining Earth.update max spikes and weak wind-target diagnostics in the latest live run, even after reducing both the model-diagnostics payload cost and the dominant particle sample0 path
annual / 365-day evidence is still missing for any long-horizon or world-class claim
world-class status still requires realism and smoothness to pass in the same browser-backed run
cycleInputs: latest runtime summary from npm run agent:summarize-runtime-log

seasonalDurabilityRecovery.tradeoffNote: Lowering thetaeCoeff to 10.5 recovered the 30-day dry-belt ratio and slightly improved the 90-day ratio as well, so the quick-versus-seasonal tradeoff is materially smaller than before.

runtimeTextureWarningFix.result: fresh reopened localhost tab no longer reproduced the startup THREE.WebGLRenderer texture-update warning

soloMinimapFix.result: fresh solo localhost rerun no longer rendered the lower-right white minimap panel

windDiagnosticsSampling.result: sampled model diagnostics reduced due-tick cost from about 30 ms to about 9-10 ms and improved Earth.update p95 to 26.32 ms, but larger unknown spikes still blocked signoff

windParticleSampleFastPath.result: an in-range bilinear fast path plus scratch-sample reuse reduced the recent mature live Earth.update p95 from 42.01 ms to 33.39 ms on the confirming rerun while lowering sample0Ms p95 from 2.60 ms to 2.30 ms; the remaining worst spikes are now mostly unknown instead of clearly particle-evolve dominated

browserObservation.note: Both fresh solo reruns stayed single-tab, kept the texture-warning and minimap fixes intact, and advanced cleanly through Day 2 while the WindStreamlineRenderer fast path lowered the repeated Earth.update p95 baseline.

defaultNextPriority: run one bounded live diagnostic cycle on the remaining unknown Earth.update max spikes before another renderer tweak; the old sample0 bottleneck is materially reduced
if that diagnostic cycle does not expose a sharper runtime/app target, return the next physics cycle to northern subtropical dry-belt moisture partitioning while keeping the faster particle sampler
rerun live verification on the thetaeCoeff=10.5 baseline after the next targeted runtime or dry-belt fix
run the annual planetary audit after the dry-belt fix holds through another seasonal follow-through
keep validation on the clean world-class checkout only
